title: Visa Bulletin — Immigration News & Alerts — ELLIS PORTER
content:
After much anticipation, the US Department of State has published the Visa Bulletin for October 2020 , which showed advancement in most employment-based categories.
Surprisingly, USCIS followed with an announcement that it will be using the “Dates for Filing” chart for accepting employment-based adjustment of status application filings in the month of October. Typically, USCIS uses the “Final Action Dates” chart for accepting adjustment of status application filings.
Date movement is slowing or has stopped in all categories.  Learn more in our analysis of the June 2018 visa bulletin.
Who are the advancers for May?  See our analysis on what lies ahead for priority dates.
EB-1 adjustment applicants need to plan for a potentially lengthy retrogression. Get our analysis of the latest visa bulletin.
The April visa bulletin brings important news for the EB-1 preference category.
March priority dates, you could say, continue to steadily march forward.  Get our analysis of the latest visa bulletin.
2018 continues with forward movement.  Downgrading to EB-3 China could be advantageous for some.
2018 starts off with no retrogression in priority dates.
The calendar year ends with forward movement in many categories.
Dates march forward at a slower pace than last month.
